description: Repo-local emergency response policy for the portable foundation. This file defines severity, halt recommendation, response checklist, and post-incident review guidance without configuring external notification services.

P0.title: Critical foundation stop condition
P0.description: Main branch, guard, CI, or workflow state is broken in a way that prevents safe continuation or risks corrupting tracked workflow evidence.
examples: main branch guard or CI fails after merge
workflow state points to the wrong session, plan, or active task
automation attempts destructive rollback, reset, or cleanup without explicit authorization

P1.title: High-risk workflow regression
P1.description: A core foundation gate, helper, or generated artifact is degraded but safe inspection and scoped remediation remain possible.
examples: guard behavior regresses on a feature branch
Taskmaster health reports invalid dependency references
work-tracking audit reports multiple active folders or stale current pointers

P2.title: Recoverable operational issue
P2.description: A report, optional validation layer, or task-local artifact is inconsistent while core mutation gates and main-branch safety remain intact.
examples: performance harness warning without strict failure

P3.title: Low-risk documentation or follow-up issue
P3.description: A non-blocking issue that should be tracked and resolved during normal task flow.

guidance: Stop implementation and switching tasks until current state is inspected.
Do not run reset, clean, restore, branch deletion, rollback, or force-push commands automatically.
Capture a rollback checkpoint before remediation if the working tree or workflow state is dirty.
Record the incident response plan and decision in the active work-tracking folder before continuing.

response_checklist.title: Capture rollback context when remediation may touch tracked state

escalation_guidance.repo_native: Record severity, current state, decisions, and next steps in the active work-tracking folder and session log.
escalation_guidance.external_integrations: No PagerDuty, Slack, email, or chat webhook integration is configured by this policy.
escalation_guidance.owner_review: For P0/P1, require explicit user review before remediation commands that mutate Git, Taskmaster, sessions, plans, work-tracking, or generated reports.

post_incident_review: Which gates or evidence caught it?
Which mutation surfaces were blocked or allowed?
What follow-up task or guard should prevent recurrence?
non_goals: No external incident service is configured.
No halt marker is written automatically.
No rollback, reset, clean, restore, branch deletion, or force push is executed.
